runs.eval_name: Write first 60 seconds for underground survival video

expectations.text: The opening immediately references or shows the underground chamber (matching thumbnail)
No 'in today's video we're going to...' or similar filler warmup
Stakes are established within the first 60 seconds (hours remaining, rules, what's at stake)
The opening plan includes specific visual instructions, not just dialogue
The 60-second plan transitions naturally into the 1-3 minute execution phase

runs.eval_name: Plan hook-to-minutes-1-3 transition with crazy progression

expectations.text: Opening 15 seconds confirms the massive slide from the thumbnail
Minutes 1-3 plan uses crazy progression (compresses multiple events, not just shows setup)
By minute 3 viewer should be emotionally invested in outcome
A 3-minute re-engagement moment is planned (spectacle that raises stakes)
Plan avoids slow setup/explanation — shows execution immediately

runs.eval_name: Plan hook-to-minutes-1-3 transition with crazy progression

expectations.text: Opening 15 seconds confirms the massive slide from the thumbnail
Minutes 1-3 plan uses crazy progression (compresses multiple events, not just shows setup)
By minute 3 viewer should be emotionally invested in outcome
A 3-minute re-engagement moment is planned (spectacle that raises stakes)
Plan avoids slow setup/explanation — shows execution immediately

notes: The skill produced perfect scores (5/5) across all three evals, demonstrating consistent application of the four-beat output format (Opening Beat, Hook Sequence, Premise Lock, Transition Signal).

The baseline (without_skill) showed the largest gaps on Eval 1 (0.40) and Eval 3 (0.40) — both of which required forward-looking structure (transition to execution phase and crazy progression) that a generic response misses.

Eval 2 (critique task) produced the smallest gap (1.0 vs 0.80) because identifying obvious retention mistakes is partially achievable from general YouTube knowledge. The skill added depth and a full rewrite script, but 4/5 assertions were satisfiable without it.

The most discriminating assertions are those requiring 'crazy progression' (Eval 3 Expectation 2), 'transition to execution' (Eval 1 Expectation 5), and 'visual confirmation' (Eval 1 Expectation 1 — baseline opened OUTSIDE the chamber, not inside it).
Eval 2's baseline failure was narrow: the baseline noted the ball pit wasn't mentioned until 25-30 seconds but framed it as a verbal timing issue rather than a thumbnail visual confirmation failure.
Without the skill, the baseline default was to start with a 'Hey guys' warmup and describe the creator standing outside/explaining setup — exactly the pattern the SKILL.md is designed to prevent.

Output quality delta is substantial: with-skill responses averaged ~4390 chars vs ~1716 chars for baseline — more than 2.5x more detailed with richer visual direction, specific dialogue, and sensory hooks.
